bullets.text: U.S.-Iran cease-fire appears fragile; Iran reportedly controls Strait of Hormuz access, keeping shipping risk premia and oil prices supported.
Iran attacks on Saudi East-West pipeline/production facilities cut kingdom oil output, highlighting energy infrastructure vulnerability and renewed supply-shock inflation risks.
Oil edges higher as Hormuz traffic doesn’t normalize post-cease-fire; market signals suggest geopolitical risk remains embedded in crude pricing.
Potential Iranian tolls/fees on Hormuz transits could monetize chokepoint leverage over ~one-fifth of seaborne oil, increasing global transport and insurance costs.
U.S. core inflation gauge remains sticky around 3%, complicating Fed easing even as war-driven energy shocks threaten second-round price pressures.
BEA reports personal income/outlays updates for February 2026, a key read on consumption momentum and inflation trajectory amid geopolitical volatility.
Q4 2025 U.S. GDP third estimate plus corporate profits and state data refine the growth/profit baseline markets use to judge recession risk and earnings durability.
Fed ends enforcement actions with several major banks, signaling remediation progress and potentially reducing regulatory overhang for affected institutions.
Warsh Fed confirmation process hits a snag with delayed hearing, injecting uncertainty into future Fed leadership and policy expectations priced into rates markets.
China factory-gate prices return to growth after three years, reportedly aided by surging oil; could export upstream inflation and alter global rate-cut narratives.
NATO strains amid Iran war and U.S. political pressure raise alliance-cohesion risk, affecting European defense planning and long-run security commitments.
Russia-Ukraine: announced Orthodox Easter cease-fire meets skepticism; U.K. reports Russian submarines spying near Britain, underscoring persistent escalation risk.
A 'super El Niño' risk plus conflict-driven fertilizer and shipping disruptions could lift global food prices, amplifying inflation pressures beyond energy.
Breakthrough water filter reportedly removes 98% of PFAS 'forever chemicals', suggesting scalable remediation potential and future regulatory/cleanup cost implications.
